name: screen.garden

releases.author: sloanelybutsurely
releases.description: 2.1.2 includes some bug fixes.

releases.author: sloanelybutsurely
releases.description: Version 2.1.1 contains fixes to our new comments feature. You can [read more about collaborative comments in Obsidian](https://screen.garden/blog/comments/) on our blog <3

releases.author: sloanelybutsurely
releases.description:
# Comments!

Markdown notes synced via screen.garden now support comments. To show all the comments in a note, run the `Show comments` command. To add a comment, select some text and either right-click and select `Add comment` or use the `Add comment` command—on mobile, it helps to add this command to the toolbar.

Comments can be made online or offline, can be replied to, deleted, and resolved. They also work on the web! You can read more about comments [in our blog post](https://screen.garden/blog/comments). Let us know what you think!

releases.author: sloanelybutsurely
releases.description: - Added a "Debug collection" command for generating useful debug info for support requests.

releases.author: sloanelybutsurely
releases.description:
- Adds realtime multiplayer collab support for Obsidian canvas: every .canvas file in your screen.garden collection is now automatically an infinite collaborative whiteboard with realtime cursors!
- Lots of other small changes and fixes.

releases.author: sloanelybutsurely
releases.description:
- lays some groundwork for a much bigger feature release that's coming soon
- screen.garden will now check for plugin updates and let you know (via status icon and home tab) when one is available
- we've make background sync queues more durable, meaning that file sync status should persist across Obsidian restarts
- fixed some bugs and typos

releases.author: sloanelybutsurely
releases.description:
## Bigger files, bug fixes

- We've increased the maximum allowed file size for non-markdown files from 20 MB to 200 MB. Give us your tired, your hungry, your massive PDFs…
- We tweaked how screen.garden syncs Markdown tables to make it play nicer with Obsidian's table editor—if you're a heavy table editor user please try it out and let us know how it feels!

As always, follow https://screen.garden/blog for our longer-form updates and other collaboration content.

releases.author: sloanelybutsurely
releases.description:
## New access commands!

We've added new commands to quickly share notes publicly or behind a password. Previously this was a web-only feature; now you can quickly jot something down and grab a read-only url with a single command or hotkey.

We also added file sync reliability checks.

Happy sharing!

releases.author: sloanelybutsurely
releases.description:
## Full vault sync and collab

- You can now connect your entire vault to a screen.garden collection.
- Doing so will sync everything in your vault—notes and files—for teams or those working solo.
- For more info and a quick guide on how to set this up, [see our blog post on full vault sync](https://screen.garden/blog/full-vault-sync/).

##  Other fixes

- We've also fixed a few small issues, including one which caused some Android clients to disconnect during sync.
- As always, [follow our blog](https://screen.garden/blog) for the latest updates on what we're working on!

releases.author: sloanelybutsurely
releases.description:
## What's changed

- Minor improvements to file uploads

releases.author: sloanelybutsurely
releases.description:
## What's Changed

- Minor bug fixes

releases.author: sloanelybutsurely
releases.description:
## File sync!

We’re excited to launch support for non-markdown file sync and exit early access into general availability.

You can read more about this feature on our blog: https://screen.garden/blog/file-sync/

releases.author: sloanelybutsurely
releases.description:
## Private collections!

You can now have collections of private notes within a team—notes that either just you can access or you and a subset of your team can  access. Read more about it [in our blog post](https://screen.garden/blog/private-collections/).

## Smaller fixes

We also fixed a bug where the editor menu might sometimes not appear and added extra handling around when your login session expires, prompting you to log in again.

releases.author: sloanelybutsurely
releases.description:
## External edit support & lowered prices

1.1.0 includes some bug fixes and support for "external edits," i.e. edits make to your notes outside Obsidian. Obsidian still has to be open, and we really prefer you edit directly in Obsidian for efficiency, but support is there just in case!

Also: we [lowered our prices](https://screen.garden/blog/even-cheaper/)! We're now the same price as Obsidian Sync, plus you get two collaborators for free.

releases.author: sloanelybutsurely
releases.description:
## Our doors are open!

Three years ago we started tinkering in our free time on an idea: what if we could have Google Docs-style collaboration right inside Obsidian? Over time that idea accreted, as all good ideas do, into a series of interlocking features: realtime collaboration, web editing, opt-in publishing, teams, seeing where your teammates are in your notes…we started building and we just couldn’t stop.

In the middle of that three-year period we released a private beta that looked, at least on the web and in the backend, pretty different from what we’re releasing today. Mostly what we learned from our private beta was that we had to keep building! That this was the kind of thing you couldn’t do partway. We’re finally at a point with screen.garden where we can throw open the door.

So, without further ado: [screen.garden is now open for registration](https://app.screen.garden/users/register) in what we’re calling [early access](https://screen.garden/blog/early-access/).

releases.author: sloanelybutsurely
releases.description:

releases.author: kevboh
releases.description:
- Adds a status bar icon to track the status of your screen.garden connection.
- Better in-UI error messages when things fail.
- Adds labels to show how many documents and invites you have remaining.
- Adds a "web" section to the document panel for easy access to each note's corresponding web address.
